dashboard.theory_card: 120 minutes of source minimum theory hours mapping.
dashboard.clinical_card: 4 recommended clinical hours are deferred; not online credit.

module0.title: NATP Module 14: Rehabilitative Nursing
module0.intro: This dry run contains Module 14 only.

acknowledgements.text: I understand this is a Module 14 dry run.
I understand clinical time is not online credit.
I understand hands-on competency is deferred.

final.title: Module 14 Review Check
final.summary: Dry-run review only; no production final exam claim.
final.pass_body: Dry-run only; no certificate or approval claim.

certificate.intro: Certificate production is not part of this Module 14 dry run.
certificate.ready_body: Dry-run complete does not issue a production certificate.
certificate.restriction: No clinical-hour credit, CDPH/TPRU approval, or online hands-on competency validation is claimed.
certificate.affidavit_text: I confirm this is a non-production Module 14 dry run.

clinicalHub.title: Clinical Support Deferred
clinicalHub.warning: Clinical practice is source-stated (4 recommended clinical hours) and not online credit.
clinicalHub.documentation_warning: Do not enter PHI. Use fictional, de-identified practice only.

scenarios.title: In-person clinical practice deferred
scenarios.body: Hands-on performance/evaluation is deferred and not validated online.

modules.source_status_flag: Generated from approved Module 14 PDF source map.
learning_objectives: Discuss rehabilitation (restorative care) and how it promotes independence and patient/resident potential
State goals of restorative care that promote independence and patient/resident potential
Describe the rehabilitation team, state its purpose, and discuss the role of the Nurse Assistant as a member of the team
List the responsibilities of the Nurse Assistant in promoting patient/resident self-care
List activities that make up Activities of Daily Living (ADLs)
List common comfort and adaptive devices and explain the purposes of each
Identify steps to prevent complications from inactivity
Identify procedures and devices used to promote mobility and ambulation for patients/residents with physical and/or visual impairment
Discuss relationship between patient's/resident's self-esteem and family involvement in care
Discuss the process of documentation and the Nurse Assistant role in care plan meeting

Trapeze; strengthens muscles and facilitates movement assisting disabled persons with adaptive devices 6. Sheepskin; avoids friction, skin breakdown F. Return demonstration
